hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., E193, K208), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target (SSTR3/5) electrostatic interactions and preserve SSTR2 selectivity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is producing candidates with strong ddG but insufficient selectivity. A more aggressive focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions is required to push Δmargin further above zero.

proposed_changes.rationale: To increase selectivity, we must disrupt conserved interactions with off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding.
These mutations are designed to break conserved hydrogen bonds or hydrophobic interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while avoiding disruption of the pharmacophore.
